_README: Single source of truth for the recorded-voice replay harness. Privacy-safe: run/start/end/text only -- NO audio lives here. Consumer contract (all three code pieces read coordinates ONLY from this file, never inline): json.load(open('tests/fixtures/recorded_voice_manifest.json')) -> {clip_dir, clips:[...], barge:[...]}. clip_dir is the LOCAL gitignored dir where the extraction tool writes clips. Each clips[] entry = {id, run, start_sec, end_sec, expected_text, rms_peak}; each barge[] entry = {id, run, start_sec, end_sec}. Filename convention every piece agrees on: the extractor writes <clip_dir>/<id>.wav (16 kHz mono 16-bit PCM) plus a sibling <clip_dir>/<id>.json. The id is the clip filename stem. Timestamps are seconds into logs/runs/<run>.wav on the WAV TIMELINE (NOT summary.json at_sec, which is on the runtime clock); the extractor adds 100 ms pre/post padding. Consumers self-skip when a source WAV (extraction) or an extracted clip (replay/tests) is absent -- so a clean checkout with no models/audio stays CI-green. Windows below were VERIFIED by replaying each through the real sherpa recognizer: the recorded final reproduces expected_text at the listed word-overlap (all >= 0.6).

clips.expected_text: Can you tell me a story
clips.note: Clean request. Verified real-ASR final 'CAN YOU TELL ME A STORY' (overlap 1.0).
Clean request. Verified real-ASR final 'TELL ME A LONG STORY' (overlap 1.0).
Clean command. Verified real-ASR final 'STOP SPEAKING' (overlap 1.0).
Question. Verified real-ASR final 'OUR LISTENING TO ME' (overlap 0.6 -- 'are'->'our').
Second long-story sample, different session. Verified real-ASR 'TELL ME A WRONG STORY' (overlap 0.8).

barge.note: Loud sustained talk-over ('can you stop / stop', RMS~0.45) for the SPEAKER_LIVE-gated barge-overlap test -- the user voice that should cut the assistant.
Sustained talk-over sequence ('stop... hey stop', RMS~0.33) -- alternate barge clip.
